flashcards.back: Observation = seeing what happens. Measurement = putting a number on it. 'Ball falls' = observation. 'Falls 10 meters' = measurement.
How close your measurement is to the true value. Accurate = correct, not just precise.
How exact your measurement is (how many decimal places). A scale might read 65.347 kg (precise!) but be wrong (not accurate).
Every measurement has limits. '5 meters ± 0.1 meters' tells you how precise the measurement is.
Tool limits, human error, environmental changes, and sometimes quantum effects. All measurements have some error.
